seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with n8n AI Agents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with n8n AI Agents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: n8n AI Agents enable powerful Agentic RAG workflows where agents autonomously plan retrieval queries, fetch from multiple sources, and synthesize attributed responses. These multi-step agent chains introduce risks like hallucinated citations, unverified source faithfulness, and recursive execution loops that can cascade errors. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify query planning accuracy, cross-source consistency, and output reliability before actions execute.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with n8n AI Agents
architectureOverview.description:
A typical n8n Agentic RAG workflow begins with a trigger node (webhook/HTTP) feeding user queries to an AI Agent node configured with planning tools. The planning agent decomposes queries into sub-queries and routes them to parallel Retriever Agents connected to vector stores (Pinecone/Weaviate) and structured data sources (PostgreSQL/Notion). Retrieved chunks flow to a Synthesis Agent that generates final responses with source attribution, often using memory nodes for conversation context.

Critical failure points occur at agent handoffs: unvalidated retrieval outputs pass to synthesis, stale memory injects irrelevant context, and tool calls expose credentials if parameters aren't sanitized. The workflow chains 4-6 agent nodes with conditional routing based on confidence scores, making end-to-end verification essential.

Vex integrates as a webhook node after each AI Agent output, validating planning steps, retrieval faithfulness, and synthesis before downstream execution.

risks.name: Workflow trigger chains causing recursive agent execution
risks.description: Planning agent generates infinite sub-query loops when confidence thresholds fail
risks.mitigation: Vex query planning verifier limits recursion depth and validates query divergence

risks.name: AI node outputs not validated before passing to action nodes
risks.description: Unverified retrieval chunks containing hallucinations propagate to synthesis agent
risks.mitigation: Vex source faithfulness checks after each retriever before synthesis handoff

risks.name: Credential exposure through agent tool call parameters
risks.description: Dynamic tool parameters constructed by agents leak API keys in vector store calls
risks.mitigation: Vex parameter sanitizer scrubs credentials from tool call payloads

risks.name: Memory window nodes injecting stale context into conversations
risks.description: Conversation memory pulls irrelevant prior retrievals, causing cross-query contamination
risks.mitigation: Vex context recency verifier filters memory by query similarity thresholds

implementation.title: Planning Agent with Vex Query Validation
implementation.description: Add Vex webhook after planning agent to verify sub-query decomposition before parallel retrieval

implementation.title: Retriever Faithfulness Verification
implementation.description: Validate retrieved chunks match planning sub-queries before synthesis

implementation.title: Cross-Source Consistency Check
implementation.description: Ensure alignment across vector, SQL, and document retrievers

implementation.title: Final Synthesis + Citation Verification
implementation.description: Verify final response faithfully represents retrieved sources

vexIntegration.title: n8n Webhook Node Configuration
vexIntegration.description: Configure Vex as HTTP Request node after each AI Agent with this payload

vexIntegration.title: Credential Sanitizer Middleware
vexIntegration.description: n8n Code node to scrub tool parameters before Vex validation

vexIntegration.title: Conditional Routing After Vex
vexIntegration.description: IF node configuration to route based on Vex verification results
